BLKOUT · A dinner series · Partnership invitation

Ivor's Table

Prostate health on the menu — for the Black queer men every other campaign misses. Four dinners. One conversation that could save a life.

The men in the gap

Black queer men
are Black men.

At more than double the population risk of prostate cancer — and reached by almost no one. Outreach to Black men works through barbershops, churches and men's groups, where we're often absent or on guard. Mainstream LGBTQ+ health talks sexual health and rarely says the word prostate. We fall between both systems — and nobody's campaign is built for the rooms we trust.

How it works

The table does the work.

Four hosted dinners, October 2026 – March 2027. A shared Caribbean and West African meal carries the conversation most of us have never had — about prostate cancer risk, our bodies, our fathers, and looking after each other. No clinic. No lecture. No leaflet first.

  • The turn — a trained voice (a nurse, a health champion, a man living with prostate cancer) joins as a guest, not a lecturer.
  • The pass — we finish and plate the dessert together, shoulder to shoulder.
  • The risk moment — everyone does Prostate Cancer UK's 30-second risk checker at the table, phones out.
  • The send-off — each man leaves with the recipe, and a dessert to carry to a man he names.

Partner with us

We have the rooms.
Bring what you have.

BLKOUT is ten years of community owned by and for Black queer men in the UK. Through our Ivor's Compass heritage programme we already reach men in their 40s to 70s — the ones for whom the risk conversation is most urgent.

A trained voiceA venueA community catererReferrals & reachYour name beside ours

What you get: a tested route into a community your channels don't reach — and the evidence to prove it.

The risk science is Prostate Cancer UK's — we've submitted Ivor's Table to their Black Health Equity programme, currently at Expression-of-Interest stage.

Ivor Cummings met the Windrush at Tilbury — and fed a generation at his Friday table.

Pull up a chair.

We're setting the table again. If your work touches Black men's health, Black queer life, food, or care — let's talk.

Set a table with us →
